item.title: Don’t let the sun go down on meme - pt23
item.description:
Sounds like a squirrel tried to steal their goodies
I started saving memes in 2020 and posting them to Instagram. It’s really been a hit with my friends and I hope you are all enjoying the backlog as I finally get around to deleting them from my phone!
item.dc:creator: notyouraveragekatie

item.title: I’m Kendra and this is my pottery!
item.description:
Reposting without a selfie (Sorry! I’m new here but I know the rule now!) Just wanted to share my pottery with y’all! I’m Kendra (she/her) Full time ceramic artist making monsters and storybook pottery in my home studio out in West Texas! Basically, I’m trying to make the Wild West a bit weirder. Happy to be here ❤️ My studio manager/ cat for tax at the end. That’s Amelia Pond, she manages what I create in the studio, drinks the clay water and has claimed at least two boxes as hers in the studio. #art #oc #creativity
My favorite studio tools
#zombie #art
In progress and prepped for glazing
Work in progress little Cyclops cup
Amelia Pond the studio #cat
item.dc:creator: hazelwildroot

item.dc:creator: victory456

item.title: Help me, Imgur. I did a thing.....Story time
item.description:
I moved to Thailand...quit my job of four years and opened my own business. I thought I was living the dream. I was teaching art in an awesome studio on a tropical paradise island. For awhile it was amazing. I put up with the utter incompetence of the school's owner because I got to swing in a hammock beachside every weekend with amazing friends from England, South Africa, Germany, France, and so many other countries. For a guy from small town Arkansas, it was so cool to be in this international atmosphere. Then Covid hit and completely decimated the island's tourist industry.
Any educators out there know that we have one of the most underappreciated professions, no matter where you work. EVERY one of the decision makers at the school had ZERO educational experience and had never taught a class. They were business first and all their input reflected that. The school was 100 percent presentation over education. Their Facebook page made the place look amazing but every experienced teacher and all the students could see through the bullshit. The school had about 80% staff turnover every year. When the island's economy crashed it got so much worse. We didn't receive raises and even took pay cuts for almost a year. They also packed our duties and classes so that every teacher was doing the work of three without compensation. We were told the money was tight and we had to work together so the school could survive...all while we watched the admin build a new sports center and spends tons of money on single events that looked good on social media.
I made it longer than anyone. At four years, I was the longest lasting employee. Every email I sent for supplies, resources, or support was ignored. I lost all passion for what I was doing. I always said that just once in my teaching career I would love to work at a place that cared more about the experience than about the bottom line. A place where the kids were enthusiastic about learning. Basically, I was tired of being a professional babysitter. I know that is part of the job, but it became overwhelming as the school waved all kids in as long as they had a check in their hand. No support for students needs: language difficulties, no counselor for mental health needs, not even an area for them to run around on breaks to get rid of all that kid energy.
I kept thinking about how these kids needed a place that wasn't just about conformity and worksheets. A place where they could work with other students at skill level instead of age level. A place where they could apply learning through hands on activities and real problem solving. So I took a SHIT LOAD of my savings and built it myself in secret. When I put in my resignation it was only then I was offered a giant raise and shown appreciation. I didn't even blink at the attempt to buy me back. I stuck to my idea and quit my job two months shy of completing my contract...something I have never done. If they would have shown just a sliver of appreciation, honesty, and support along they way, I would have stayed for years in that awesome studio space. but instead
I created Club Loco. This is the logo/emblem for the club. I know that the name and logo aren't professional but that's the whole point. I'm the anti-professional and the meaning behind the name is actually more clever than just "crazy". It's the only international youth club on the island. I didn't think it would be instantly successful but I thought as least some of the parents and students at my school would come because they know what I'm capable of as an educator. While I did have some interest, it was not was I was hoping for and I became a little deflated. Soooo many people kept telling me not to give up because I had a great and unique idea and I was doing it for the right reasons. The major problem is that while I am an experienced educator who has taught almost all subjects in three different countries, I am a shitty businessman who has a problem promoting myself. I HATE social media with a passion and didn't get the word out like I should have.
This is the duck pond at my club. We have access to amazing animals and foliage that surround the place.
This is pool number one. When I was looking for a space to build my dream, the landlord was so excited that I was a foreigner who wasn't just going to build another bar. She saw my passion and loved that I wanted to give back to the Thai community and help it after the hit from Covid. She gave me access to her luxury resort adjacent to my rental space. I wanted to give opportunities to Thai students at public school who might not have the chance to visit a facility like this.
The greenhouse is going to give my students an opportunity to grow their own food and learn about sustainability and conservation. The landlord has been so supportive (which I'm told is unusual because she is a tough as nails business woman). She has discounted the rent and is letting me fill the properties division wall with student murals.
This is the clubhouse/classroom. It is completely stocked with art supplies of all kinds, computers and PlayStation with educational games and software for graphic design, interior design, film and photo editing, and just about anything a student needs to follow their creative ideas. We have tubs filled with cans, tools, junk, cardboard, and plastic so they can engineer and build things. All of the resources are available at no extra fee.
The idea is simple. Most activities offered on the island are based on hours. Tutoring, sports, art, etc...all are offered based on a one hour time frame. They charge anywhere between 500 and 1000 (15 to 30 dollars) an hour. I charge 750 for an unlimited time. If I am open for 5 hours that day they can come for 1 or five. After we work on their homework or studying they can choose their own direction and activities.
The parents and students that come are all amazed with the concept and are SUPER supportive. I have one member whose parents have given me their accounting software they use for their own business, invited me to lunch in their home and coached me on starting a business in a foreign country, and are even donating a ping pong table. Another parent is amazed because they thought their son would panic after two hours and call to be picked up. He stayed for six hours and has returned every Saturday since. His father said I have a natural gift and is working to introduce me to several people on the island who can promote me.
I have wanted to quit so many times. I was so close. Every time I was about to liquidate and try to recoup some of my losses, something happened that made me keep going. This is the hardest and most worthwhile thing I have ever done. Unfortunately I am learning that, while I am a dedicated and passionate teacher (the only thing in my life I have confidence in) I am complete shit at business. But I'm trying.
This is the nature pen. While I hate to see animals retained behind fences, these are well taken care of. These deer were so hesitant at first, but my students have been regularly visiting and feeding and now they run to the fence when we come. Students are just filled with awe when the deer eat from their hands. Here is where I ask for help. I don't want donations. All I need is more social media visibility. If any of you could just go the clubs Facebook and Instagram and like or follow or whatever that would be a big help. You can mute the posts right after. Clublocophuket on both. You can see some of the videos and activities there to learn more about me. Thanks for reading and wish me luck! Success or failure, I am happy I did this. As my German student's father told me "No matter what happens, be proud of what you built because you had the balls to try".
item.dc:creator: Mybellybuttonsmellsweird

item.title: We did the thing!!
item.description: Most viral? Wow thanks. Send Bobs. Every Bob is beautiful.
item.dc:creator: BenNeumann742
